codeReview.summary: The commit cleanly adds a focused cross-surface test suite and wires it into the test target. Coverage for VAL-CROSS-001 and VAL-CROSS-002 is strong and directly exercises upgrade propagation and bounded no-op refresh behavior. One non-blocking coverage gap remains in how 'reconciliation path' usage convergence is simulated.

issues.description: The VAL-CROSS-007 usage convergence tests model the reconciliation path by directly inserting exact rows (`insertAndRefresh`) rather than invoking scheduled reconciliation/backfill logic. This bypasses reconciliation-specific merge/delta behavior and could miss regressions where scheduled processing diverges from direct upsert semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The indexing-efficiency-worker skill requires reading `.factory/library/user-testing.md` in step 1, but the reviewed worker transcript skeleton shows reads for mission/agents/validation/services/architecture and source files only, with no `user-testing.md` read.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure step 1) vs transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 93172fc4-0309-4b66-b769-50e03651264f (extracted `file_path` entries show no `.factory/library/user-testing.md`).

summary: Reviewed feature m3-cross-surface-upgrade-and-refresh-efficiency by inspecting feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit f3f970e diff. The added tests are coherent and mostly aligned with the feature goals; no blocking defects were found. Logged one non-blocking reconciliation-path coverage gap and one skill-procedure/shared-state observation.
